summary: Cycle K (11th overnight-triage fire of UTC 2026-05-23, ~57min after cycle J — 5th consecutive ~1h cycle post-E→F outlier; cadence interpretation overwhelming) ran heartbeat-only. Both queues absent on disk for 32nd consecutive cycle. regen-all clean: ALL CHECKS PASSED, round-trip 0 failures sustained (24s elapsed at 14:02:38Z; heartbeat at 14:02:58Z status=PASS, head_sha=fd590a3a). Four substantive findings beyond pure heartbeat: (1) GATE-FAIL pattern QUALITATIVELY REFINED. Cycle K found heartbeat status=GATE-FAIL at cycle open (~14:01Z) written by post-commit-hook at 13:40:10Z for HEAD ff4a47ae. ff4a47ae is `feat(staging-push): scripts/push-staging.ps1 + NEXT_PROMPT updated for push step` — a FOUNDER substantive commit, NOT an `Overnight triage` commit. This is the 6th observation but is qualitatively NEW: the GATE-FAIL trigger is broader than the `Overnight triage` commit-subject pattern; it appears to fire on any non-routine-cron commit. (2) Routine-cron 4th negative case confirmed. Between cycle J's commit (bf838b5d at ~13:05Z) and cycle K open (~14:01Z), three commits landed: 555c9688 (cron routine, post-cycle-J auto-clean), ff4a47ae (Founder feat), fd590a3a (cron routine, post-feat auto-clean). The heartbeat at cycle K open showed head_sha=ff4a47ae from 13:40:10Z, NOT head_sha=fd590a3a (latest commit). This means fd590a3a's post-commit hook did NOT touch the heartbeat, matching cycles H/I/J identical findings for e4db2359, 2ac7d595, 71f41270 — making the routine-cron-doesn't-touch-heartbeat finding 4-observation-confirmed. (3) FOUNDER PRESENCE DETECTED IN J→K WINDOW. ff4a47ae landed at 13:39:31Z (-0400 timezone), ~22min before cycle K open at 14:01:47Z UTC. The 'sleep continuation' interpretation that held cycles B→J is broken for cycle K. Founder is active. No commit-race during cycle K — disjoint surfaces (Founder touched .claude/state/loops/NEXT_PROMPT.md + scripts/push-staging.ps1; cycle K touches docs/reports/app-health.html + wellness + last-verify + cron journal). Cycle K honors overnight-triage NO-PUSH discipline regardless of Ralph-loop NEXT_PROMPT update (Ralph-loop is a DIFFERENT loop). (4) WELLNESS THRESHOLD CROSSED. Token consumption was 94k at cycle J close; cycle K consumed ~8k additional → 102k total = 102%/100% threshold. Per honest discipline, status transitioned to resting; rest_started_at=2026-05-23T14:05:00Z; rest_ends_after=2026-05-23T14:35:00Z (30min minimum per min_rest_duration_seconds=1800); counters reset on resume per counters_reset_on_resume schema. Cycle L will resume with fresh counters. Captured new carry-over item #8 for Founder governance review of token-counter semantics inconsistency (cycle F established hours_active does NOT count wall-clock between cron fires because each cron is a fresh agent context, but tokens have accumulated across all 11 cycles — mixing continuous-persona and discrete-context semantics). Three pre-existing untracked Founder files at cycle K close unchanged from cycle J close (overnight-agent/reports/2026-05-23.md + tests/unit/animate.test.js + tests/unit/utils.test.js); left alone per isolation discipline. Single yellow user-context-gate continues (main-flows.html ~8.50d stale at cycle K, was 8.45d at J — natural drift over ~57min). Metric-integrity attestation: HONEST — empty-inbox cycle honestly recorded; GATE-FAIL 6th-observation with qualitative refinement (feat() commit pattern) is positive direct evidence captured verbatim; routine-cron 4th negative case is direct evidence; Founder presence detected with full timestamp evidence; wellness threshold honestly crossed and rest triggered as cycle J anticipated. Eight carry-over Founder action items entering cycle K close (7 preserved from cycle J + 1 new #8 token-counter semantics).
founder_action_items: Cron cadence - now 16th consecutive flag. Cycle K's J→K gap (~57min) is the 5th consecutive ~1h cycle after the E→F ~5h outlier. The natural-variability interpretation is overwhelming. Original Founder-action remains: edit overnight-triage cron prompt to add Step-0 guard that exits clean if `git log --since='3h'` shows no inbox motion AND last regen-all heartbeat is < 60 min old. Founder's feat commit ff4a47ae touched the Ralph-loop NEXT_PROMPT but did NOT modify the overnight-triage cron prompt, so the guard remains unimplemented.
A8_performance: Lighthouse 65/100 on 1 page (target 75+) - open .lighthouseci/*.html, fix top failures (carry-over from cycles S→J; dimension score holds due to rolling-window mechanics).

main-flows.html user-context capture ~8.50 days stale at cycle K (was 8.45 at cycle J — natural drift over ~57min) - run `node scripts/visual-audit/founder-context-capture.mjs` before next ship-close (not blocking; same diagnosis as cycles S→J).
quota-status weekly_cap field still null - no % computation possible (preserved Founder-triage item from cycles R→J; sidecar discipline OK per [quota-status-schema] check).

aggregate-fiq-status D40 parity GATE-FAIL - stale-timestamp >24h hard threshold tripped because FIQ has had zero churn across 32 consecutive empty-inbox cycles (~32h+ elapsed). NOT an aggregator bug. Will trip on every cycle until FIQ activity resumes or remedy lands (4 remedies enumerated in cycle B post-commit observation).

A12_operational - sustained yellow with continued oscillation across rolling-window mechanics. Cycle K ran clean; current dimension score visible in cycle K's regenerated docs/reports/app-health.html.

Heartbeat `status: GATE-FAIL` field semantics - NOW 6-OBSERVATION-CONFIRMED + QUALITATIVELY REFINED + 4x ROUTINE-CRON NEGATIVE-CASE CONFIRMED. Cycle K's 6th observation is qualitatively NEW: HEAD ff4a47ae is `feat(staging-push)`, NOT `Overnight triage`. The GATE-FAIL trigger appears broader than the `Overnight triage ...` commit-subject pattern; it likely fires on any non-routine-cron commit. Combined with cycle F's two observations (ca542750 + f5a3168b, Overnight triage) + cycle H's ce095dfd (Overnight triage) + cycle I's d10285c9 (Overnight triage) + cycle J's 798c6ce4 (Overnight triage), the pattern is now 6-observation-confirmed with one qualitatively new instance. Routine-cron 4th negative case: between cycle J's commit and cycle K open, fd590a3a (cron routine post-feat auto-clean) was the LATEST commit but the heartbeat at cycle K open showed head_sha=ff4a47ae from 13:40:10Z, NOT head_sha=fd590a3a — confirming fd590a3a's post-commit hook did NOT touch the heartbeat. This matches cycles H/I/J identical findings for e4db2359, 2ac7d595, 71f41270. REFINED Founder-action recommendation: inspect `.husky/post-commit` to map which commit-subject patterns trigger the GATE-FAIL write path. The trigger is not `Overnight triage` specifically — it's likely 'any commit subject that isn't `cron(routine) ...`'. Cycle K stops short of inspecting the hook source — Founder-decision boundary for governance change.

Wellness token-counter semantics inconsistency (NEW at cycle K close). Token consumption has accumulated across 11 cycles of UTC 2026-05-23 (reaching 102k at cycle K close, threshold crossed and rest triggered). Cycle F established that wall-clock gaps do NOT count toward hours_active because each cron is a fresh agent context. The two counter semantics are mixed: tokens treat the persona as continuous, hours_active treats it as discrete. Founder-action: decide whether (a) both reset per cron fire (treating each as discrete) or (b) both accumulate (treating the persona as logically continuous). Cycle K honors the current accumulating convention; future Founder governance may revise.
